description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the Docker image registry.gitlab.com/isf-traffic/isf-traffic.gitlab.io with three stages: build, notify, and deploy. In the build stage add a build:release job that runs ./update.py -sl, stores the public/ directory as an artifact (expire 6h, untracked, on_success) and runs only when the commit tag matches ^isf-traffic-.* or $FORCE_RELEASE is set; also add a build:update job that runs ./update.py -su, archives public/ (expire 1d, untracked, on_success) and runs only when $DAILY_UPDATE is set. In the notify stage add a notify:release job dependent on build:release that lists the public directory (ls -l public/) and runs ./notify.py -l, executes on_success, runs only for tags matching ^isf-traffic-.* and is skipped if $NO_NOTIFICATION or $FORCE_RELEASE are present; also add a notify:update job dependent on build:update that lists public and runs ./notify.py -u, runs only when $DAILY_UPDATE is set and is skipped if $NO_NOTIFICATION is set. Finally, in the deploy stage add a pages job that lists public, publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, runs on_success, runs only when the commit tag matches ^isf-traffic-.* or $DAILY_UPDATE or $FORCE_RELEASE is set, and is skipped if $NO_DEPLOY is set.
